title: Algae Agar Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7dkdp","text":"This protocol details the making of algae concentrate agar for the growth of bacteria from Douglas Lake.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"858fn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9fvpg","text":"Protocol for class project in Microbes in the Wild: Advanced Environmental Microbiology Lab (EEB 447) course at University of Michigan Biological Station and on campus. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":30,"length":71}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: During the peak of a cyanobacterial bloom, bring a 100μm mesh out into the water. Collect as many colonies as possible using the mesh and collect them into three sterile 50 ml falcon tubes.

Sterilize a conventional blender with 10% bleach. Rinse with 1-2L MilliQ water Pour the contents of the three 50 ml falcon tubes into the blender. Blend on high for 2 minutes. Add the contents of the blender into a sterile 1L round glass media bottle. Add 15 grams of agar to the media bottle. Bring up the mixture to 1L with MilliQ water Steam autoclave at 121˚C for 30 minutes on Liquid Cycle 2 Pour agar into sterile petri dishes and allow to set. Store plates at 4˚C.
creator: Dylan J Baker
